pi_items.source: Verified against task description (line 5-6) and verifier.py (line 83) - this is the name specified for the global shared library to be configured

GitHub API query (https://api.github.com/repos/jenkins-infra/pipeline-library) confirms this is a valid public repository. Web search and GitHub API verify the repository exists and is maintained by Jenkins Infrastructure project.

GitHub API query confirmed default_branch is 'master' for jenkins-infra/pipeline-library. Web search results show multiple files from master branch (Jenkinsfile, vars/infra.groovy, etc.) confirming master branch exists and is active.
Verified against task description (line 5) which specifies 'Create a new Pipeline job named shared-lib-test'. Consistent across export_result.sh (line 50) and verifier.py (line 35).

Verified as standard Jenkins Declarative Pipeline syntax for importing shared libraries (https://www.jenkins.io/doc/book/pipeline/shared-libraries/). The annotation matches the expected_lib_name. Checked in verifier.py line 119.

privileged_info_summary: The task configures a global Jenkins shared library named 'jenkins-shared-lib' using the real jenkins-infra pipeline library from https://github.com/jenkins-infra/pipeline-library.git at the master branch (confirmed as default branch via GitHub API). A test job named 'shared-lib-test' is created with the standard Jenkins annotation @Library('jenkins-shared-lib') to import and test the configured library. All metadata claims are consistent with the actual repository structure and Jenkins pipeline conventions.
